page.title: Rate Limiting
page.lead: Rate limiting restricts how many requests a client can make within a time window. It is a key layer to protect your API against abuse, brute force, and uncontrolled traffic spikes.

importance.title: What is it used for?
items: Reduce brute-force attacks on login and sensitive endpoints.
Avoid server saturation from excessive traffic.
Protect expensive resources such as search or exports.
Improve overall stability under high concurrency.

middleware.title: Basic usage of rateLimit() middleware
middleware.description: You can limit requests with the built-in <mark class="docs-highlight">rateLimit</mark> middleware.

sensitiveRoutes.title: Example on sensitive routes
sensitiveRoutes.description: It is recommended to apply stricter limits on authentication or account recovery endpoints.

keyGenerator.title: Customize keyGenerator
keyGenerator.description: By default, IP is used (considering <mark class="docs-highlight">trustProxy</mark>). If you want quotas per authenticated user, define <mark class="docs-highlight">keyGenerator</mark>.

skip.title: Skip routes with skip
skip.description: Use <mark class="docs-highlight">skip</mark> to exclude routes or specific conditions from counting.

customHandler.title: Custom response with handler
customHandler.description: You can adjust <mark class="docs-highlight">statusCode</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">message</mark>, and define a <mark class="docs-highlight">handler</mark> to return your own error format. You can also control headers with <mark class="docs-highlight">standardHeaders</mark> and <mark class="docs-highlight">legacyHeaders</mark>.

multiInstance.title: Multi-instance deployments
multiInstance.description: In multi-instance deployments, provide a shared store (for example Redis) by implementing <mark class="docs-highlight">RateLimitStore</mark> and passing it in <mark class="docs-highlight">store</mark>.
multiInstance.warn: If you do not use a shared store, each instance will count requests separately and global limits will lose accuracy.

bestPractices.title: Best practices
items: Use stricter limits for login, account recovery, and high-cost endpoints.
Enable <mark class="docs-highlight">trustProxy</mark> only if your API is behind a trusted reverse proxy.
Keep <mark class="docs-highlight">standardHeaders</mark> enabled to improve observability in clients and gateways.
In cluster or multi-node serverless deployments, use a shared <mark class="docs-highlight">store</mark> to avoid inconsistent limits.
